title: Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS): Interviews of 27-Year-Olds 1986
description: The data are part of the Jyväskylä Longitudinal Study of Personality and Social Development (JYLS), in which the same individuals have been followed over 30 years. At this research stage, data were collected through interviews probing 12 different themes. The recorded interviews were numerically classified according to the themes only afterwards, when it was possible to observe the whole range of responses.
